explanation: The property expresses that always, if the robot has a self-recovery strategy (selfRecoveryExists), then it should explain that strategy to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy). This directly captures the natural language specification that 'if the robot has a self recovery strategy, explain it to the operator.'

The property expresses that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available (selfRecoveryExists is true), then it must explain this plan to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy becomes true). This directly encodes the 'if ... then ...' relationship indicated in the requirement.

This LTL property formalizes the requirement: 'If the robot has a self recovery strategy, explain it to the operator.' Here, 'selfRecoveryExists' means there is a self-recovery strategy available, and 'explainRecoveryStrategy' means the explanation is given. The template G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3) is used so that always, if the robot has a self-recovery strategy (selfRecoveryExists), then it must explain the recovery plan (explainRecoveryStrategy) to the operator. This directly captures the conditional relationship in the original requirement.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available (selfRecoveryExists), it should make sure to explain this plan to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy). This is a general implication applied globally, which best matches the natural language statement of conditionally requiring an explanation when the capability exists. 'G (selfRecoveryExists -> explainRecoveryStrategy)'.

The LTL property ensures that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available (selfRecoveryExists), it must explain this strategy to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy). This fits the requirement stated in the natural language: 'If the robot has a self recovery strategy, explain it to the operator.' The implication is captured globally to ensure this requirement is always upheld during the system's execution.

The natural language specifies that if the robot has a self-recovery strategy, then it should explain this strategy to the operator. This is best modeled as a global implication: whenever 'selfRecoveryExists' holds (the robot has a self-recovery strategy), 'explainRecoveryStrategy' (the robot explains its plan for self-recovery) should occur. The corresponding template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', where 'bool_exp1' is 'selfRecoveryExists' and 'bool_exp3' is 'explainRecoveryStrategy'. This specification always enforces that the explanation must happen whenever there is a recovery plan.

The specification expresses that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available (selfRecoveryExists), it should ensure that it explains its plan for self-recovery to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy). The implication is captured globally (G) so this must hold at all times: wherever selfRecoveryExists holds, explainRecoveryStrategy must immediately hold as well. This matches the 'If... then...' structure of the natural language input.

This LTL property states that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available (selfRecoveryExists), it must eventually explain the strategy to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy), within a bounded time interval. The implication captures the 'If ... then ...' structure of the command, while the event 'F[0,N_DURATION]' ensures that the response (the explanation) does not happen indefinitely far in the future, which fits the natural language requirement.

The property encodes that whenever the robot has a self recovery strategy (selfRecoveryExists is true), it must explain it to the operator (explainRecoveryStrategy becomes true at the same time). This is a straightforward mapping of the natural language condition to a safety property using implication.

The property encodes that whenever the robot has a self-recovery strategy available ('selfRecoveryExists'), it must (at the same time or in the future) explain this strategy to the operator ('explainRecoveryStrategy') at least once within a given time window (N_DURATION). This uses the template (G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))), corresponding to 'Whenever selfRecoveryExists holds, explainRecoveryStrategy must eventually (within N_DURATION) be true.' This matches the intent of the input natural language: 'If the robot has a self recovery strategy, explain it to the operator.'
